author: terencechain
content: i think we resolved that one in <#874767108809031740> (co location issue), but let me know if it still persists

author: terencechain
content:
how aggressive is Lodestar requesting payload by range? we are getting spammed by the requests but i cant tell if its our bug or not.. Tons of:
```
Serving execution payload envelopes by range request count=1 handler=execution_payload_envelopes_by_range peer=16Uiu2HAkw7e8kyt5H8A53kMtVjAGR67LRaHzCnYHLdqRhRxjm3wi startSlot=15744
```
i've pushed some fixes to prysm's devnet3 branch, but having a really hard time to sync to head of the chain given very limited working nodes to serve valid payload and block. we'll see if we can sync to the head soon

author: nflaig
content: I am sure this is a bug on our end, we are way to aggressiv, been debugging rate limit issues most of last week, we need to fix this

author: nflaig
content: it wasn't as big of an issue when we had more nodes serving data last week

author: terencechain
content:
thanks.. 
another comment lodestar is the only healthy nodes left, once it bans us, there's no way to sync 🙂

author: nflaig
content: I have shutdown our dev infra nodes, they are very likely banned also by the other lodestar nodes due to spam, but at least you should see less spam on your end

author: potuz
content: <@363800010518822915> is the leading theory that Prysm went to sync by the watchtower and then isolated itself by downscoring peers that didn't return Payloads?

author: terencechain
content:
- Prysm downscore -> bar peers when they rpc request us over rate limit. (afaik we are the only one that do this)...  Lodestar (the only healthy nodes through out) got banned because of that. So prysm lost lodestar
- Teku for some reason never respond payload by range along side block by range. So we cant sync with teku, and we would downscore and ban teku as well because of the empty response
- Lighthosue / grandine are not healthy right now

I think where it went bad is we downscore and ban peers if they request RPC too frequently vs other clients handle more gracefully so its easy for prysm to lose its good peers even under happy case
